world.title: The Philippines’ hard balancing statecraft won’t deliver the South China Sea Code of Conduct
world.description: Written by Pheng Thean. If the Philippines seeks a realistic pathway towards a functional COC — and to preserve ASEAN’s credibility as a neutral convening platform — it must complement its instruments of statecraft with more targeted diplomatic adjustments.

science.title: Moby Dick 'ship sinking' sperm whales caught headbutting on camera
science.description: New research from the University of St Andrews reports sperm whales headbutting one another. The behavior was captured on film and described scientifically for the first time, confirming accounts by 19th century mariners of sperm whales using their heads to deliberately push and strike objects, occasionally even sinking ships, thereby inspiring Herman Melville's classic tale Moby Dick.

ai.title: backed Lace raises $40 million for advanced chipmaking equipment tech
ai.description: Lace, a Norwegian chipmaking startup, secured $40 million for its helium atom beam lithography. This technology promises chip designs ten times smaller than current capabilities. It could enable unprecedented advancements in AI processors. Lace aims for a pilot test tool by 2029. This innovation could revolutionize semiconductor manufacturing.
